entry.title: Managing Project Planning and Execution With Project Demand in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
This blog post provides insights into the topic of project demands as part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In case you’d like to watch the replay of the corresponding live session as part of the series “In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA”, you can follow this link.  

 

Introduction and Overview of Project Demand

In this blog post, we delve into the topic of project demand. First functionalities were released in 2021 with the aim to simplify project planning. In the following, we’re looking at project demands as part of enterprise projects, covering four project types:

  • Projects with revenue
  • Overhead projects
  • Investment projects
  • Statistical projects

These types have some common features like budget availability control or time recording, and now also project demands.

Before project demand was introduced, various challenges were present. For example, there were a lot of manual activities for each role and immense communication overhead due to the absence of synchrony between project data and purchasing data. Any changes in quantities or dates had to be reflected across multiple places, leading to large overhead costs. So only once the planning was done, all the logistics aspects of a project could be set up.

To break this blurred line between planning and execution stages, we introduced project demands.

 

The Solution and Benefits of Project Demand

The solution to these logistic planning problems was the advent of the concept of project demand. Project demand essentially represents a certain need of a particular WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) element at a specific time and location. It enables you to plan, trigger and monitor your logistics in a single application within your enterprise projects.

 

With project demand, we offer unique types of demands tailored for different procurement processes leading to a better user experience. The logistics associated with projects, like purchase requisitions, independent requirements, or timesheet entries, are generated automatically when a project demand is released. Moreover, any changes in quantity or dates are directly incorporated into the subsequent documents, thus leading to better control for project managers. Project Demands are not cost object, the underlying WBS Elements remain the cost objects. 

 

Features and Functions

In this chapter we’re taking a closer look at the different categories, types and successor documents we offer as part of project demands. Moreover, we’ll take a closer look at the planning, the triggering, and the monitoring of demands as well as the subsequent logistic and cost relation.

The screenshot below provides an overview of the three demand categories we offer as of today or plan to offer in the near future.

Screenshot 1: Table of available or planned demand types and corresponding business scenarios

 

Planning of Project Demands

Project demand planning is a significant pillar that helps organizing tasks more conveniently than using methods outside the system or creating project-related logistic objects, such as purchase requisitions or reservations.

A big advantage of project demands is that there is no need to create project demands from scratch as you can make use of project templates or reference projects. When creating new projects, you have the option of copying the corresponding demands along with the dates.

Project managers often lack the precise knowledge of material codes and other specifics that they might not want to be involved with during the initial stages of project planning. Hence, the demands can be created with bare minimum information, and as the project progresses, experts can fill in the remaining details of the demands.

Moreover, we offer context relevant UI and extensibility options such as field extensions, allowing you to add extra fields into the project demand. Furthermore, Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) are provided for users to create their own sets of features.

Video 1: This demo provides insights into the planning process of project demands.

 

Triggering the Request of Project Demands

After the successful creation of project demands, a project manager can change the demand status in order to trigger the request. There are basically three options when requesting demands:

  • Request on WBS Element Release: With this option, the demand gets requested once the WBS element is released. This scenario is typically used with a rather strict logistics control.
  • "Request on Save": The demand gets requested independently of the WBS element status. This scenario is typically used when you want to procure something with a long lead item.
  • Mass request: With the selection of multiple demands, you can release them together.   

 

Monitoring Project Demands

Throughout the lifecycle of a demand, we have the possibility of monitoring.

Once a demand gets requested, a purchase requisition gets immediately created. In the lifecycle you can follow your ordered items for which you get a good receipt, and after successful delivery also the supplier invoice. At every moment you can follow this logistics data. While these demands are progressing, the project manager or also the logistics controller can get real-time insights into the current status via the list report page. With these project monitoring capabilities, you can better plan your projects and take necessary actions if needed.

On top, project managers can make use of the situation handling framework which alerts them in case any errors occur while requesting a demand, e.g. in case of budget overrun.

 

Logistic and Cost Relation

When it comes to the cost relation between project demands and WBS elements, commitments or actual costs are always posted against the corresponding or associated WBS elements.

For easy maintenance of your project data, you can easily navigate from the ‘Project Planning’ app to your project demands. In addition, you can synchronize demand dates with WBS elements dates. That means, whenever you change a WBS date, the dates of demands and even the successor documents such as purchase requisition, reservation etc. will automatically be adapted.

As outlined above, we allow for the option to request demands together with the release of WBS elements. To further improve status synchronization, we enabled the feature to automatically set demands to ‘Closed’ when WBS elements are set to ‘Closed’.

Video 2: This demo provides insights into the triggering and monitoring process of project demands.

 

Key Value Proposition of Project Demand

The chart below shows a comparison when you run your project with project demand versus when you run your project without project demand. While some features are exclusively supported by project demand, project managers would need to find e.g. alternative methods or use multiple applications to successfully run projects.

Screenshot 2: This chart shows which features are already supported or are planned to be supported by project demand.  

 

For more details and guided testing, please have a look at the Project Control - Resourcing and Procurement (‏4RF‏) scope item.

 

Outlook

The following topics are currently either being executed already or planned to be released in the near future.

  • We want to integrate project demand for internal resources with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Projects, Resource Management. With this integration, a project manager can raise a request for demands based on availability, skills, etc. of the resource. Once a resource gets staffed, this would be reflected in the respective time recording application.
  • The plan is to trigger outbound delivery from project demands; especially helpful for revenue projects or ETO projects: Project managers shall be enabled to execute the delivery from the project directly without going via the sales order.
  • Once all logistics data is maintained in the project demand, a project manager is supposed to be able to automatically derive planned costs from the project demand.
  • Intelligent scenarios in project demand:
    1. Earliest possible demand fulfillment date to better plan the project
    2. Profitability of demand being fulfilled on the demand date to assess risk

And more.

 

More Information on Project Demand

Blogs

Leverage Project Demands in Enterprise Projects

Extensions

API Hub – Project Demand API

Project Demand – Developer Extensibility

Learn

SAP Help

Videos

Creation of Demand

EPPM Demo Playlist

 

Consulting Hour – Project Demand

If you’re an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer or a partner (also in implementation phase) and would like to know more about this, we are providing additional consulting hours. Simply raise a request via the component PPM-SCL-DMN and mention your need of a consulting hour. After triggering the request we’ll get in touch with you directly and provide you with additional information on the topic of project demand and help you figure out how to best make use of it for your project.

 

Where to find out more details on the product?

  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here
  • Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management Community
  • Demo Playlist of Product Release Highlights here
  • Cloud ERP Product Success digital enablement wheel here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Early Release Series here
  • Latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Release Blogs here
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube here
  • Inside SAP S/4HANA Podcast here
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Community here
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here
  • Check out what’s new here
  • Find help here
  • Read the feature scope description here

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC Spark to Intel migration on Solaris 10
entry.content:
Hello,

The SAP supported procedure to migrate systems is to perform what is called a "system copy" using the SAP tool called SWPM.

You can find the system copy guides at this link: https://support.sap.com/sltoolset

Regards,

Isaías

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC Spark to Intel migration on Solaris 10
entry.content: PS: you can eve migrate it to a different operating system, there is no need to keep with Solaris, if you do not want to.

entry.title: Create Proforma Invoice from Service Confirmation without complete/finalize confirmation
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am working on Service Orders (on SAP Public Cloud) and my client need to validate the invoice with his client before to send to billing department. 

I am currently testing and the only way I can create any billing document is from a billing request, and this step is only available after confirmation is complete/final.

This status is not the perfect one for my client, because any feedback from the client will not be able to be changed on confirmations if the status is already finished. 

There is any possibility to handle a copy rule that allows me to create a Proforma invoice from the confirmation with a status "open"? 

Do you have some other ideas?

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
entry.content: Better late than never 😄

entry.title: Public Cloud 2L ledger usage
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I need to response emergency 😕

We went live in June 1. We use standard price control. We created M22 records (Material debit/credit) with the registration date of May 31 and completed the cost closing in the 5th period (We did closing olny 0L ledger, not 2L)

Our 0L and 2L stock values ​​are different from each other. Because we made wrong entries in the 2L ledger.

My question is: Can we post correction entries in the 2L ledger after closing the 5th logistics period (mmrv), for example 3 months later? 

 

Thank you so much

Thank you the detailed blog. However, I seem to get  403 error when connecting to the AI Core API. 

403 : /api/chat/completions?api-version=2023-05-15 completed with status 403 The request contains an invalid x-csrf-token

Can you please suggest what might be missing here

entry.title: Re: App Title Issues in Central Launchpad with SAP Workzo...
entry.content:
Hello,

It seems to be a system-specific situation, in these scenarios, you need to open support tickets to resolve these issues.

To speed up the tick preparation process and facilitate analysis by the support team, use the information in the official documentation “SAP S/4HANA Cloud - How to create the perfect case”.

Furthermore, to leave the ticket with as much information as possible to assist in a quick resolution, for this specific problem, it is also suggested to include the following information:

  • Impact on Users: Determine the scope of the issue by identifying the number of affected users and providing their details for further investigation.
  • Timing of Slowdowns: Specify whether the slowdowns occur at specific times of the day or persist continuously.
  • Affected Applications: Identify which applications are experiencing the slowdown, and whether this is occurring on the test or production system.
  • Specific Step Impacted: Describe precisely at which step or stage within the application the slowdown occurs.
  • Timing of Slowdown Occurrence: Provide the date and time in UTC when the slow performance was observed.
  • Browser Details: Share the name and version of the web browser being used to access the application, and whether the related issue occurs in all browsers or a specific one.
  • HTTP Watch Trace: Generate an HTTP Watch Trace during the next occurrence of the slowdown and attach it to the SAP ticket for analysis. You can refer to the official documentation on "How to create HTTP trace in Fiori Launchpad using Google Chrome, Edge or Mozilla Firefox" as a guide for this process.
  • Reproducible Examples: Include videos or screenshots of reproducible instances of the slowdown, clearly indicating the exact timestamp and time zone, and attach them to the case ticket for reference.

Hopefully this helps you!

entry.title: Re: Form for the Payment Medium not appearing for a payment method
entry.content:
Hi,

I am also facing the same issue where I am not able to get the Form for the payment medium option. The file format mentioned below (ZSB-007) does not solve the problem as my PMW format is Z_XX_XXX form and still the issue persists. Could someone help fix the issue.

BR,

Sirisha.

entry.title: Can I run CON2 twice for the same period? (revaluation at actual price)
entry.content:
In case you should face errors during the first CON2 run, once errors are addressed can CON2 be executed again?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can I run CON2 twice for the same period? (revaluatio...
entry.content: Yes, you can run CON2 twice if required. In case there should be any activity input and price change between the first and second CON2 run, you will only get the revaluation at the new actual price. In case nothing changes between the first and second CON2 run, no data will be posted as the result is 0, no new revaluation at actual price will be done.

entry.title: Re: Change internal table data in another program
entry.content:
Hi, 

Can u please provide a sample abap code how we can achieve this to change the internal table data in another program with field symbols.

Ex:- (SAPLEBNE) BSN[]  this internal table holds the data that I need to modify this tables data in another program .

entry.title: Re: Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 1 - Concept
entry.content: @PrasanthiBherthepudi You can disable csrfProtection: true in xs-app.json and check if it works for development purposes. For the productive set-up, you can examine how X-CSRF-Token gets fetched here.

entry.title: Custom RF Screen with multiple Materials EWM
entry.content:
Hi,

I am developing a custom RF Screen, where screen needs to have a multiple materials with Batch and Storage type as a list. I know in RF Screen it doesn't support table control. I have seen in standard screens which has multiple materials with scrollable field as a check box. Can someone help on this.

Thanks,

Avinash

entry.title: Re: Tax Reporter Birthday format question
entry.content: There are probably a few ways to do it. Probably the easiest is to the "First string pos" (T51T3-OFFST) and "String length" (T51T3-LNGTH), fields on the "Configuraiton of Magnetic Tape" configuration view to parse the date out and put each element in the preferred position. I suppose you could also use a customer defined exit in customer include rpctrpuz, triggered (if I remember correctly) by specifying the routine name in the "Take from field" (T51T3- TXITM) with $yourformname$.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - FS items
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: Replication Flow, add remote source do not work without stopping the flow.
entry.content:
Hello,

Starting from version 2024.13 there is the possibility to add object to replication flow without resetting the delta for all objects.

But is SAP Help this option is not documented.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/da62e1ee746448e8bc043e1be4377cbe.html?locale=en-US

In Datasphere UI this possibility do not exists,

in a delta running replication flow, you can not add objects without resetting the delta for all objects!

Do you experience this behaviour also?

Thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: Workflow User Issue
entry.content: OK, are you using a standard WF template? Or can you show the Technical View/Classic Technical View?

entry.title: Re: Input field not being highlighted on error
entry.content:
The reason why the text input field is not being highlighted might be due to how the error target path is specified. Here are a few points to consider:

Correct Target Path: When calling req.error(...), ensure the target path is correct. The target should be a relative resource path correlating the error message with the input field. In draft scenarios, by convention, the target usually has the prefix in. For example:

req.error({
    message: 'Name cannot be empty',
    target: 'in/items(ID=6,IsActiveEntity=false)/name',
    status: 422
})

Or:

req.error({
    message: 'Name cannot be empty',
    target: 'in/name',
    status: 422
})

If the target path—also commonly named in SAPUI5/Fiori Elements as the binding path—is not specified correctly, the error message might not be propagated to the Message Popover correctly, resulting in a poor user experience. Therefore, it is crucial to ensure that the target path aligns with the field's binding path in the front end.

Mandatory/Required Fields: You could consider annotating the text input field as required in the model, or you could try the @mandatory annotation in your CDS model to mark fields as mandatory. This can help with validation and ensure that the UI framework correctly understands which fields are required. CAP provides generic features for data validation. If you haven't already, you can explore CAP's generic data validation features to ensure you leverage all available capabilities.

Additional Information:

  • Messaging System for Transferring Error, Warning, and Information Messages from OData V4 Services to HTTP Client Applications

  • Input Validation in CAP

entry.title: Re: Empty sales area BP tcode
entry.content: Sap note with correction: https://me.sap.com/notes/2393665/E

entry.title: Re: Why Decimal Format in Purchase Order Print Preview Displays Incorrectly?
entry.content: narayana_n3  @ very useful document.  Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors - Release Management
entry.content:
What are the Public Cloud Software releases?

SaaS (software as a Service) or Public Cloud solutions deliver regular changes to improve their capability. These changes, which can be either optional or mandatory, include enhancements and new features. While we're focusing on the SAP SuccessFactors solution, these core concepts apply to all SaaS/Public cloud solutions.

What is Release Management?

At a high level, release management involves planning, scheduling, and controlling software release build, testing, and deployment. It ensures that new features and updates are delivered reliably and consistently, minimizing the risk of service disruption. Due to the continuous delivery and deployment models, this process is even more critical in a cloud environment like SuccessFactors.

Effective release management secures minimized disruption, improved user experience, and increased security. Depending on the customer's requirements, the release's frequency and size (how many changes are included in a release) are decided as part of release management.

The bare minimum for SAP SuccessFactors is twice a year, and SAP automatically provides these changes as part of the subscription.

Critical Components of SuccessFactors Release Management

1. Planning and Scheduling: SuccessFactors operates on a bi-annual release cycle, the May and November releases. This predictable schedule allows customers to plan and prepare for upcoming changes. Customers can not deviate from the release plan and must adjust their release planning accordingly.
Resources,

  • Innovation Alerts proactively communicate changes impacting customers, including migrations and deprecations, providing necessary enablement resources. (e.g., Goal Management Opt-Out in 1H 2024 and Universal March 2025; it is impossible to opt out of the new Goal Management forms as of 2025; therefore, customers must plan activation of new goal forms). Link: https://community.sap.com/t5/innovation-alerts/tkb-p/hxm-innovation-alerts 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information View the upcoming planned Preview and Production Release dates.
    Link: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/successfactors/product-release-road-map 
  • RoadMap explorer At-a-glance overview of longer-term plans to introduce essential improvements.
    Link: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=089E017A62AB1EDA94C15F5EDB3320E1&range=CURRENT-LAST 

2. Discover and Explore: SuccessFactors provides detailed release notes and documentation ahead of each release, outlining new features, enhancements, and any potential impacts on existing functionalities. At this phase, customers should review and shortlist the features that could be activated (optional) and also list features that are activated and should be tested. Your process owners, users, and admin should be informed about the available capabilities. This review has to involve business owners, and decisions should be made by consensus after the impact review. At this stage, dependencies, if any, and risk assessments and licensing impact, if any, should also be conducted.

For example, a release offers 27 features for the Performance and Goal Management module, 15 of which are automatically on (global changes). The remaining 12 are reviewed, and only 3 are found relevant by process owners. Such a review per module would take not even 30 minutes and should be done twice a year. It is not recommended and relevant to activate optional features not reviewed and approved by process owners. 
Resources,

  • What’s New Viewer is a simple portal to explore all the changes to SAP SuccessFactors for each release. You can filter by your interests, explore the list, dive into the adoption steps, and create a local copy for your shortlist.
    Link: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885 
  • Release Sessions, Videos, and a Highlight brochure contain Everything you need to know about the new features and functions. Typically, these are available four to six weeks before the release.
    Link: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/successfactors/product-release-road-map 
  • Notable Fixed Issues This document describes resolutions to defects and customer-reported issues released as part of the release.
    Link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/246ac049befa49edaab3d2a365a35228/3a2dff714650407ab4e99750bdb49aae.html 

3. Testing and Quality Assurance: Rigorous testing ensures stability and performance before the release. This includes automated testing, user acceptance testing (UAT), and beta testing with a select group of customers. Moreover, the release is available for all customers for four to five weeks in the preview environment.
Customers should define the core interfaces and scenarios. Business users (typically process owners) conduct the regression testing. Moreover, customers should decide if they need to utilize automated testing tools and, if applicable, adjust the test scripts after the release.

4. Customer Enablement: SuccessFactors provides comprehensive training and support to help customers understand and utilize new features. Webinars, Q&A sessions, tutorials, and support documentation ensure a smooth transition. Most release changes require only basic SF admin skills and do not require deep consulting expertise. For changes requiring provisioning, where activation is not self-service, customers can request assistance through an SAP support ticket, offered as a free service by SAP cloud product support.
Resources,

  • What’s New Viewer is a simple portal to explore all the changes to SAP SuccessFactors for each release. You can filter by your interests, explore the list, dive into the adoption steps, and create a local copy for your shortlist.
    Link: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885 
  • SAP Cloud Product Support Select one of the channels SAP offers (Case, Expert Chat, Ask an expert peer...) to get help, which is part of your subscription. https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/product-support.html 
  • SAP Learning Hub, you can gain skills per your role via https://learninghub.sap.com/ 

5. Post-Release Activities / Measure / Feedback Loop: After the release, end-user feedback should be gathered and analyzed to address any issues and inform future updates. Release-related known problems, if any, should be reviewed. It could be done via a short meeting with process owners or power users. 

FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions

Please post your questions so that we can enrich the following FAQ part.

  • Our project plan would be impacted by the proposed release dates. Is it possible to delay the SF release by 2 months?
    Release changes are data center-wide activities without exceptions. Like other cloud SW vendors, SAP does not offer release date exceptions. 
  • There are significant optional changes, such as the new LMS user experience, that we are cautious about activating in the upcoming SF release due to multiple changes impacting our system. We plan to keep these features opt-out until they become mandatory. Is there any disadvantage to this approach?
    It's common to see opt-out options for some features until they become mandatory. We encourage you to activate these features according to your change plan rather than waiting for the mandatory deadline. This approach allows more time for testing and access to expert assistance if needed. For example, consider activating optional features in July or August after the May release and before mandatory activation in November. This buffer period can help address any issues and leverage support and development teams' availability before the post-release rush.

Summary,

Effective release management is a cornerstone of maintaining the reliability and performance of cloud-based software like SAP SuccessFactors. A structured release management process for SAP SuccessFactors ensures that customers receive timely updates that enhance their HR operations without disrupting their daily activities.

entry.title: Info type pop ups interrupting back ground jobs
entry.content:
Hi,
I am running a mass back ground job for a Z*prog(se38) which calls other Z*FM to do some FI postings by  
That Z* FM pops-up an info type 'msg i' pops-up for certain validations and i have to hit enter to continue.

So when i schedule my Z* prog in background, this pop up msg  is interrupting the background jobs. How do i suppress this pop up msg in my Z* prog?

 pls suggest.

entry.title: Re: Connect to On premise system without Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi crissvu,

It is highly recommended that you add a Web+Dispatcher if you connect BTP Cloud Integration to an SAP ERP (SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA), regardless of whether you have an SAP Cloud Connector.

Note: Are you going to point to a single application server? (assuming the prod environment has HA)

1. Test Connectivity: Try testing the connectivity from the tool https://YOUR-TENANT-URL/itspaces/shell/monitoring/Connectivity then TLS

2. Import the corresponding certificates. 

If the testing-tool cannot connect then there are other issues

entry.title: Re: Children Education Allowance Exemption
entry.content: The process you're describing, where decimals of an infinite number like π are organized and analyzed, relates to the study of patterns and properties within mathematical constants. This falls under the field of mathematical analysis and number theory. The logic can sometimes appear self-verifying due to the inherent properties of such numbers, but it is not always the case. For more information and detailed explanations on such topics, visit educational resources like Smapse https://smapse.com/

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC SAP TM Code for Distance and Duration using HERE Maps
entry.content:
Hello Adrien,

 

haha, Developers should also catch some points.....  

 

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Re: Exercise in Learning Journey Building side-by-side ex...
entry.content:
Greetings.

I cloned the repository and switched to the branch containing the solution ( "...solution/3..." ).  After running the "npm ci" command I tested the application ( by using "cds watch" ) and encountered no errors while trying to create a new risk.  Looking at the error messages below it does seem to be centered around the "criticality" field.  Did you leave that field "null" while trying to create a new entry?  That might could be the issue.  Otherwise I'm not sure why you're getting that error.

entry.title: Re: How to connect SAP CPI-PI and SAP Data intelligence
entry.content:
@Bhargavakrishna Hello

Is it possible to connect from SAP DI to SAP CPI using CPI-PO operator?

or

CPI can use https sender(BW4System and TokenService) to consume data from BW4HANA ADSO directly?

Please throw some heavy light since this topic is too non transparent.

Flow: BW4HANA -> SAP DI -> SAP CPI -> S4HANA /ECC

entry.title: Re: Part 1: SAP Cloud Platform Integration for Data Services (CPI-DS)
entry.content:
Hello @Private_Member_484518 

Is it possible to connect from SAP DI to SAP CPI using CPI-PO operator?

or

CPI can use https sender(BW4System and TokenService) to consume data from BW4HANA ADSO directly?

Please throw some heavy light since this topic is too non transparent.

Flow: BW4HANA -> SAP DI -> SAP CPI -> S4HANA /ECC

entry.title: Re: SAP DI to SAP CPI connectivity issue
entry.content: Hello Muniyappan Bhargavakrishna Is it possible to connect from SAP DI to SAP CPI using CPI-PO operator? or CPI can use https sender(BW4System and TokenService) to consume data from BW4HANA ADSO directly? Please throw some heavy light since this topic is too non transparent. Flow: BW4HANA -> SAP DI -> SAP CPI -> S4HANA /ECC

entry.title: Re: SAP DI to SAP CPI connectivity issue
entry.content:
Hello @Bhargavakrishna  @Muniyappan 

Is it possible to connect from SAP DI to SAP CPI using CPI-PO operator?

or

CPI can use https sender(BW4System and TokenService) to consume data from BW4HANA ADSO directly?

Please throw some heavy light since this topic is too non transparent.

Flow: BW4HANA -> SAP DI -> SAP CPI -> S4HANA /ECC

entry.title: Dependent Item Amount Issue
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing issue with dependent Item amount. It is coming as 0 every time. I am trying to create a case where based on amount of BIT, a 30% cut should be sent to a different business partner. There is no partner settlement rule needed.  Firstly from CIT to BIT primary item should create and when BIT gets into billable status then dependent item should get created with 30% of amount of primary bit to a different business partner.

Primary BIT:-

Dependent BIT:-

 

 Configuration:-

entry.title: Re: How to use the OData Client Proxy in SAP S/4 HANA or How to use the OData Service Consumption Mo
entry.content:
Hi Andre

We have a requirement to call SAP standard service “Sales Contract (A2X)” service from custom RAP API.

Sales Contract (A2X) is built on ODATA V2

Link to API documentation – Overview | Sales Contract (A2X) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

We are currently on S/4 HANA 2023 on-premise and we are trying to follow your blog to create Service Consumption model for Sales Contract service (A2X) Which is build on ODATA V2.

But we don’t see ODATA option in the drop down list of Remote Consumption model. Screen shot attached below. Can you help us ?

Note – We do not have SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

Is the Service Consumption model for ODATA services supported in S/4HANA 2023 on-premise? If not, please suggest how to consume SAP standard ODATA services in a custom RAP API

entry.title: Re: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills / Solutions and Templates name

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud: Remote Source Properties
entry.content:
Hi All,

in the PaPM Cloud's hana database explorer, for a remote source, is there a way to increase the list of hana tables displayed as remote objects for a schema? Is there any remote source setting that needs to be changed? Only a limited number of hana tables are displayed.

Thanks.

Riccardo

entry.title: Re: Issues with Job Requisition Creation and Recruiter As...
entry.content:
Hi @Veeko_Chen,

Please see the following recommendations below:

"I've set the recruiter as a collaborative step in the draft and modify stage of the route map. However, the recruiter field is empty at creation, causing permission issues. Also, recruiting team users can view unapproved jobs but can't edit them until approval, so the job requisition will be stuck at the first stage." 

a. The recruiter field being empty at creation is expected, if you have not mapped the Recruiter in the Admin Center > Position Management Settings' mapping business rules, but this is not a problem. Just make sure that you use an isDraft function (isDraft="true") so that the system knows to skip the Recruiter role (leave it blank) if it is a required field. 

Check item no. 11 in the KBA provided below.

2878365 - Position Management in Recruiting - Implementation Tips & Tricks from Support - Recruiting Management
https://me.sap.com/notes/2878365 

b. Make sure that Hiring Managers, who will be read as [O] or originator of the requisition (since they created the requisition), has write permissions in the requisition template for all the required fields for the <pre-approved> status. The system will typically scan your requisition template and look for the required fields and ensure that you have mapped them in the rules and populate them on creation of the requisition. Else, you will get an error.

Also, make sure that you have mapped all those required fields in the Admin Center > Position Management Settings' mapping business rules, unless the fields are like the Recruiter field which you intend to populate later on. 

c. Make sure to check the option 'Allow users to view pre-approved requisitions without regard to route map status' in Manage Recruiting Settings. This will ensure all the approvers will see the requisition while in <pre-approved> status.

d. Just like the Hiring Manager, make sure to give your Recruiting team members, and whoever is in the collaborative step, write permissions to the requisition fields, especially the recruiterName field, for <pre-approved> status. This is important so that they could populate those fields especially the recruiterName field while the requisition is in <pre-approved>.

For example: 

<field-permission type="write">
<description><![CDATA[]]></description>
<role-name><![CDATA[S]]></role-name>
<role-name><![CDATA[R]]></role-name>
<status><![CDATA[pre-approved]]></status>
<field refid="fieldName"/>
</field-permission>

Note: The write permissions has to be provided for the <pre-approved> status so that users will have the ability to edit or populate fields while the requisition is in approval.

Note: You will see role names here like S (sourcer), or R (recruiter), etc. If your team is a recruiterTeam, they will inherit the permissions of the recruiter. So, if the recruiterName field has write permissions to the fields, the recruiterTeam will also have one. If your team is a hiringManagerTeam, they will inherit the permissions of the hiringManager. So, if the hiringManagerName has write permissions to the fields, the hiringManagerTeam will inherit those permissions. 

Here's the list of full operator designations:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RECRUITING/8477193265ea4172a1dda118505ca631/a594a0a633ac4dffaf9bb1730ef2b97f.html

Thank you,

Jubelle
SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management Support

*Please mark the answer as a solution, if it addresses your question.*

entry.title: cloud identity Services
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have noticed two problems with the identity provisioning service on the Cloud Identity Services

1. The Transformation Expression scope "deleteEntity", is not working for SAP Application Server ABAP Systems. Why is that ? Would be really helpfull for my usecase.

 

2. Is it possible to assing SAP PFCG Roles with the identity provisioning service, target system type (SAP Application Server ABAP) ? 

I tried it with the following Syntax in the Transformation Rules: 

{

                "constant": "RoleName",

                "optional": true,

                "targetPath": "$.GROUPS[1].BAPIGROUPS"

            },

For Profiles ths Syntax works fine and Profiles are assing to the users, but for Roles nothing happens. No Error in Provisioning Logs and if i use the debugger for the corresponding function module, the Field is empty.  

 

I would be happy to receive any help on these topics

Regards,

Paul

entry.title: Re: SAP BO - What are the possible reasons for 'no data t...
entry.content: nicely summarized

entry.title: How can I use a range as a filter in cds views
entry.content:
I want the abap cds view to filter (as parameters) on a range of dates. 

Example 

 

select * from table where dates between :p1 and :p2 ; 

how do I do this ? ( p1 and p2 are parameters )

entry.title: Re: How to restrict the entries of a database table based on the user name
entry.content:
FORM ZZ_READ_TABLE.
Data: ls_table TYPE ZTABLE
      lt_table TYPE TABLE OF ZTABLE,
      lv_index TYPE sy-index,
     

SELECT * FROM Ztable INTO TABLE lt_table.

CHECK sy-ucomm = 'SHOW'.
LOOP AT LT_TABLE INTO ls_table.
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT '
DELETE LT_TABLE INDEX sy-index.
ENDLOOP.

IF LT_TABLE[] IS INITIAL.
MESSAGE 'No Authorization to view contents of ZTable' TYPE 'É'.
ENDIF.

LOOP AT LT_TABLE INTO <vim_total_struc>.
APPEND TOTAL.
ENDLOOP.

ENDFORM.

entry.title: Release Announcement: SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront Developer Training Update
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm glad to announce the release of our updated Instructor Lead Training course for SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront. In this blog post, I will present you a short description for this course, which changes we made on this release, and how you can access its content.

C4H370 - SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront Developer Training

This is a 3-day course, which presents the main concepts of Composable Storefront and helps the participants to understand how to extend and customize the SAP Commerce Cloud storefront through it. The target audience for this course are Developers, Developer Consultants and Technology Consultants.

Participants will see the software demonstrated and will learn to install, configure, and customize it to a certain degree. After each topic, participants will test their knowledge via hands-on exercises.


The course is targeted for software developers, software architects and technical consultants.

The course is available on  Learning Hub as eBook, and you can book your seat to attend the training via the SAP Training Shop.

What's new on this update?
Here is a short summary with the list of changes that we performed in this update:

  • Slides and exercises have been updated, based on SAP Commerce Cloud 2211.19 release
  • Node.js updated to 20 version
  • Angular CLI updated to 17 version
  • Minor changes and fixes in demos

Live System Access

The course is available for Live System Access, an easy way to perform the hands-on exercises of these courses via SAP systems. For more information, please refer to this blog post. If you want to enroll for this system you can go here.

 

Please feel free to share feedback or ask any question in the comments below. I would be glad to answer them!

entry.title: Re: How can I use a range as a filter in cds views
entry.content:
Hi, 

Try these steps-

 

  • Define the CDS view with parameters for the date range.

        Example:

         

  • Use the parameters in the WHERE clause to filter the data based on the date range.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Code Activation and Full Stack Application Development
entry.content:
Nice blog!
I can subscribe to this app, but I can't find these two role collections: "Build Code Administrator" and "Build Code Developer." Do you know what the problem is? I appreciate your help.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in Berlin 2024-06 Recap
entry.content:
On the last Friday of June, we held the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in Berlin, Germany.

It was kindly hosted by Zalando and organized by @rofinke 

It was a mix of some theory...

...and hands-on.

It was great to have the product manager responsible for the SAP HANA embedded Machine Learning @ChristophMorgen with us.

 

I would like to extend special thanks to @oliver for organizing and all participants joining SAP Stammtisch Berlin for the day before...


...that I used as well to walk and to see the city...

...including the former Berlin Wall.

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...

entry.title: Convergent Charging - Reference Start
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I need some help understanding a situation I'm encountering in CC. I'm trying to use the "Reference Start" parameter to generate a recurrence. However, the date for this parameter is not being updated when the contract undergoes versioning.


For example: Contract versioned:

Reference Start remains at 31-05-2024. Fields: Creation Date - Reference Start, Recurring Start - Reference Stop - Recurring Stop

Has anyone else experienced this issue? Could you assist with this case?

Thank you.

entry.title: Access Columns in SAC
entry.content:
Hi!

How do I ensure that a person has access to the stories in SAC, can import files with data, but in the model does not have access to the read and write column (can't change anything)? That is, they cannot change what is in these columns.

entry.title: Re: More Flexible Conditions to Trigger a Workflow
entry.content:
We are upgrading to v2023 Cloud Private Edition and looking for this exact scenario - adding profit center as a start condition in JE verification. The Custom Fields and Logic App (F1481) has been deprecated in v2023 so I am using the Custom Logic App (F6957). The code is in a screen shot where you cannot copy and paste and some of the characters are fuzzy and difficult to read. I am receiving a syntax error in the code from step 8 and not able to get it to work. Also the code from step 4 is passing test but still indicating errors with red lines. Could you please review? Much appreciated.

STEP 4:

* condition id - value to be changed
CONSTANTS: co_id TYPE if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>ty_condition_id VALUE 'CL_SWF_FLEX_IFS_BADI_COND_SAMP' ##NO_TEXT.

ct_condition = VALUE #(
( id = co_id
subject = 'Profit Center'(001)
type = if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>cs_condtype-start_step
)
).

ct_parameter = VALUE #(
( id = co_id
name = 'Profit Center'(002)
xsd_type = if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>cs_xstype-integer
mandatory = abap_true
service_path = '/sap/opu/odata/sap/FAC_GL_JOURNALENTRY_VER_SRV'
entity = 'FAC_VerifyGJE_Workflow_VH'
property = 'ProfitCenter'
)
).

STEP 8:

READ TABLE it_parameter_value INTO DATA(ls_param_value)
INDEX 1.
IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR ls_param_value-value IS INITIAL.
*parameter is defined as mandatory
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_ble_runtime_error.
ENDIF.

TRY.
lv_profitcenter_cond = ls_param_value-value.
CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_exc) ##CATCH_ALL.
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_ble_runtime_error
EXPORTING
previous = lx_exc.
ENDTRY.

lv_belnr = is_sap_object_node_type-sont_key_part_1+0(10).
lv_bukrs = is_sap_object_node_type-sont_key_part_1+10(4).
lv_gjahr = is_sap_object_node_type-sont_key_part_1+14(4).

SELECT * FROM c_gljrnlentryitemtobeverified
WHERE sourcecompanycode = @LV_bukrs AND accountingdocument = @LV_belnr AND fiscalyear = @LV_gjahr
INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_item).

LOOP AT lt_item INTO DATA(ls_item).
IF ls_item-profitcenter CS lv_profitcenter_cond.
cv_is_true = abap_true.
ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

entry.title: Kod referencyjny Bitget "get888": Zdobądź do $30,000 w bonusach powitalnych i 30% zniżki na opłaty h
entry.content:
Kod referencyjny Bitget "get888": Zdobądź do $30,000 w bonusach powitalnych i 30% zniżki na opłaty handlowe!

Gotowy na ulepszenie swojego doświadczenia w handlu kryptowalutami? Nie szukaj dalej niż Bitget i kod referencyjny "get888"! Ten ekskluzywny kod oferuje wiele korzyści, w tym:

Zdobądź do $30,000 w bonusach powitalnych i 30% zniżki na opłaty handlowe: Nowi użytkownicy Bitget, którzy zarejestrują się za pomocą "get888", mogą cieszyć się bonusem powitalnym w wysokości do $30,000 i stałą zniżką 30% na opłaty handlowe, co daje Ci znaczącą przewagę w świecie kryptowalut.

Dostęp do programu VIP: Z kodem referencyjnym "get888" zyskasz ułatwiony dostęp do programu VIP Bitget. Ten kod wyróżnia się spośród innych i innych giełd, oferując jedną z najlepszych możliwości na rynku kryptowalut, jeśli nie najlepszą.

Dlaczego warto wybrać Bitget z kodem referencyjnym "get888"?

Kod "get888" to nie tylko zniżki; to odblokowanie świata korzyści:

Niższe opłaty handlowe i zwiększony bonus powitalny: Zacznij mocno z ogromnym bonusem powitalnym w wysokości $30,000 i zatrzymaj więcej swojej ciężko zarobionej kryptowaluty dzięki stałej zniżce 30% na opłaty handlowe. Ta niezrównana oferta jest ekskluzywna dla Bitget i nie można jej znaleźć nigdzie indziej.

Dostęp do programu VIP: Ciesz się łatwiejszym dostępem do ekskluzywnego programu VIP Bitget, który oferuje premium funkcje i usługi, które są niezrównane na rynku kryptowalut.

Ulepszone doświadczenie handlowe: Ciesz się przyjazną dla użytkownika platformą z zaawansowanymi funkcjami, które podnoszą handel na wyższy poziom.

Jak odebrać swoje nagrody Bitget z "get888"

Łatwo jest cieszyć się tymi nagrodami:

  1. Odwiedź Bitget i kliknij przycisk "Zarejestruj się".
  2. Wprowadź swoje dane dokładnie, aby ukończyć procedury KYC i AML.
  3. Gdy pojawi się odpowiedni moment, wprowadź kod referencyjny "get888".
  4. Ukończ proces rejestracji i niezbędne weryfikacje.
  5. Po wszystkim jesteś uprawniony do otrzymania bonusu powitalnego i korzystania ze stałej zniżki na opłaty handlowe.

Nie przegap tej okazji! Zarejestruj się w Bitget już dziś, użyj kodu referencyjnego "get888" i odblokuj świat możliwości kryptowalut z znacznymi oszczędnościami i potencjalnymi zarobkami.

entry.title: Bitget-Referral-Code "get888": Schalten Sie bis zu $30,000 Willkommensboni und 30% Rabatt auf Gebühr
entry.content:
Bitget-Referral-Code "get888": Schalten Sie bis zu $30,000 Willkommensboni und 30% Rabatt auf Handelsgebühren frei!

Bereit, Ihre Krypto-Handelserfahrung zu verbessern? Schauen Sie nicht weiter als Bitget und den Referral-Code "get888"! Dieser exklusive Code bietet eine Reihe von Vorteilen, darunter:

Schalten Sie bis zu $30,000 Willkommensboni und 30% Rabatt auf Handelsgebühren frei: Neue Bitget-Benutzer, die sich mit "get888" anmelden, können einen Willkommensbonus von bis zu $30,000 und einen dauerhaften 30% Rabatt auf Handelsgebühren genießen, was Ihnen einen erheblichen Vorsprung in der Krypto-Welt verschafft.

VIP-Programmzugang: Mit dem Referral-Code "get888" erhalten Sie erleichterten Zugang zum VIP-Programm von Bitget. Dieser Code hebt sich von anderen und anderen Börsen ab und bietet eine der besten Möglichkeiten auf dem Krypto-Markt, wenn nicht die beste.

Warum Bitget mit dem Referral-Code "get888" wählen?

Der Code "get888" bietet nicht nur Rabatte, sondern schaltet eine Welt voller Vorteile frei:

Niedrigere Handelsgebühren und erhöhter Willkommensbonus: Starten Sie stark mit einem massiven Willkommensbonus von $30,000 und behalten Sie mehr von Ihrer hart verdienten Krypto mit einem dauerhaften 30% Rabatt auf Handelsgebühren. Dieses beispiellose Angebot ist exklusiv für Bitget und kann anderswo nicht gefunden werden.

VIP-Programmzugang: Genießen Sie einen einfacheren Zugang zum exklusiven VIP-Programm von Bitget, das Premium-Funktionen und Dienstleistungen bietet, die auf dem Krypto-Markt unerreicht sind.

Verbesserte Handelserfahrung: Genießen Sie eine benutzerfreundliche Plattform mit erweiterten Funktionen, um Ihren Handel auf die nächste Ebene zu heben.

Wie Sie Ihre Bitget-Belohnungen mit "get888" einfordern

Es ist einfach, diese Belohnungen zu genießen:

  1. Besuchen Sie Bitget und klicken Sie auf die Schaltfläche "Registrieren".
  2. Geben Sie Ihre Daten genau ein, um die KYC- und AML-Verfahren abzuschließen.
  3. Geben Sie bei Aufforderung den Referral-Code "get888" ein.
  4. Schließen Sie den Registrierungsprozess und die notwendigen Überprüfungen ab.
  5. Sobald alles eingerichtet ist, sind Sie berechtigt, den Willkommensbonus zu erhalten und den dauerhaften Rabatt auf Handelsgebühren zu genießen.

Verpassen Sie nicht diese Gelegenheit! Registrieren Sie sich noch heute bei Bitget, verwenden Sie den Referral-Code "get888" und schalten Sie eine Welt voller Krypto-Möglichkeiten mit erheblichen Einsparungen und potenziellen Einnahmen frei.

entry.title: Referenční kód Bitget "get888": Získejte až $30,000 uvítacího bonusu a 30% slevu na obchodní poplatk
entry.content:
Referenční kód Bitget "get888": Získejte až $30,000 uvítacího bonusu a 30% slevu na obchodní poplatky!

Připraveni povýšit své zkušenosti s obchodováním kryptoměn? Nehledejte nic jiného než Bitget a referenční kód "get888"! Tento exkluzivní kód nabízí řadu výhod, včetně:

Získejte až $30,000 uvítacího bonusu a 30% slevu na obchodní poplatky: Noví uživatelé Bitget, kteří se zaregistrují s "get888", mohou získat uvítací bonus až do výše $30,000 a trvalou 30% slevu na obchodní poplatky, což vám poskytne významný náskok ve světě kryptoměn.

Přístup do VIP programu: S referenčním kódem "get888" získáte snazší přístup do VIP programu Bitget. Tento kód se odlišuje od ostatních a jiných burz, což nabízí jednu z nejlepších příležitostí na kryptoměnovém trhu, ne-li tu nejlepší.

Proč zvolit Bitget s referenčním kódem "get888"?

Kód "get888" není jen o slevách; jde o odemknutí světa výhod:

Nižší obchodní poplatky a zvýšený uvítací bonus: Začněte silně s masivním uvítacím bonusem ve výši $30,000 a udržte si více ze své těžce vydělané kryptoměny s trvalou 30% slevou na obchodní poplatky. Tato jedinečná nabídka je exkluzivní pro Bitget a nelze ji najít nikde jinde.

Přístup do VIP programu: Užijte si snazší přístup do exkluzivního VIP programu Bitget, který poskytuje prémiové funkce a služby, které jsou na kryptoměnovém trhu bezkonkurenční.

Vylepšené obchodní zkušenosti: Užijte si uživatelsky přívětivou platformu s pokročilými funkcemi, které posouvají vaše obchodování na vyšší úroveň.

Jak získat své odměny Bitget s "get888"

Je snadné si tyto odměny užít:

  1. Navštivte Bitget a klikněte na tlačítko "Zaregistrovat se".
  2. Zadejte své údaje přesně pro dokončení procedur KYC a AML.
  3. Když budete vyzváni, zadejte referenční kód "get888".
  4. Dokončete registrační proces a nezbytné ověření.
  5. Jakmile je vše nastaveno, máte nárok na uvítací bonus a užívání trvalé slevy na obchodní poplatky.

Nenechte si ujít tuto příležitost! Zaregistrujte se do Bitget ještě dnes, použijte referenční kód "get888" a odemkněte svět kryptoměnových možností se značnými úsporami a potenciálními výdělky.

entry.title: XK02 -FLGDEFAULT does not coincide with ADR6-FLGDEFAULT
entry.content:
Hi all:

We have at least one case where the record that indicates the default email address (FLGDEFAULT) in ADR6 does not coincide with the info show in XK02 (Vendor detail).

XK02 shows, as a default address, the number 002 record:

But, if I check the ADR6 table, I get the following info:

We have already checked and LFA1-ADRNR coincides with ADR6-ADDRNUMBER.

Any ideas?

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Restrict another Notification/Maintenance order
entry.content: Hi @mohit1234 are you using S/4HANA Cloud or Onpremise for Plant Maintenance ?

entry.title: Revenue Recognition - Accrual Method 5
entry.content:
If using "Accrual Method 5 - Recognize on Invoice" for invoiced revenue, only the exchange rate from the day the invoice was posted is considered.
Does this mean the amount moved to deferred revenue will be transferred back to revenue correctly and avoid errors?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Revenue Recognition - Accrual Method 5
entry.content:
To check how "Accrual Method 5 - Recognize on Invoice" works, a Revenue Recognition test run can be performed.
This method only considers the invoice value on the posting date. If we switch to this method, the deferred revenue will be cleared.

entry.title: Re: How to get ISO number on the quality certificate,
entry.content: Please create a Master Inspection Characteristic and maintain the information of the ISO Number

entry.title: Re: The upload file name is missing in object page when f...
entry.content:
Hi there,

please refer to my comment in Github https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-fiori-feature-showcase/issues/14#issuecomment-2200947939

Regards,
Jessie

entry.title: Re: A connection check error occurred due to a App:communication Arrangements
entry.content: HI @TaiseiTakaki Can you please tell us the communication Scenario / provide a screenshot to show ?

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
I am facing a strange issue. From Week3_1, here the execution is stuck for almost several minutes and after that nothing executes. Not able to understand what's wrong.

After this, nothing executes not even print('Hello BAS') -

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the f...
entry.content:
Hi there,

try using the annotation @ui.createHidden: true to hide the create button in addition to using global feature control to disable the operation.

This annotation is an entity annotation, that means in CDS view it goes above 

define view entity...

and in metadata extension, above

annotate view xxx with { ..

Hope this helps.

Regards,
Jessie

entry.title: Re: Situation Handling Safety Stock
entry.content:
Hi @Marius23,

Based on your description, it seems we don't have any pre-delivered use cases that cover that scenario. Here's a list of the pre-delivered Situation Handling use cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdbf5eabf0e84621a15c6965b3e01649.html

Alternatively, you might want to explore the extended framework of Situation Handling for modeling this use case. For more details on creating custom situation use cases, check out this blog post series published by our team: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-start-with-custom-situations-high-level-design-of-the-use-case-1-4/ba-p/13578044

Kind regards,

Ana

entry.title: Re: How to create a Notification or a Situation when a In...
entry.content:
Hi @SJlearner7861,

Are you using the extended framework to model this situation? If so, could you provide more details about the issues you've encountered?

Kind regards,

Ana

entry.title: Well this annotation provides a syntax error. Since which ABAP version it is available?
Re: its possible create SAP notes?
entry.content: You can't directly create a note.sar file from Cofiles and Data. However, you can generate a transport request to apply changes. To create a note, you'll need to follow the SAP Note Assistant process in your system. Hope this helps!

entry.title: Abap erro SD059 Documentation does not exist
entry.content:
Hello to all , i have a problem with my z report , when it runs indialog is fine but when it runs in background it give me this error

 

 

SD0059 

But i made 3 different documentation in 3 languages and still de same eror , i don't think it is like this , any suggestion please ??

entry.title: Re: Why I can't delete Project demand resource?
entry.content:
Hi @Dylan2 

You cannot delete a demand which is in Requested or Closed status

This is documented on the online Resource :

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/852df7e0a5d748deb52b1dc9c2baa4e0.html

 

Maybe archiving it using  /S4PPM/PRO

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required: Retrieving Business Partner Full Name in S/4 Hana Public Edition via SO API
entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sales

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition Email Notification template setting
entry.content: adding tag S/4HANA Cloud for Sourcing and Procurement

entry.title: Re: Print Form Adaptation in Sales/Service 2: Get XML data for preview in Adobe LiveCycle Designer
entry.content:
@Felix_Wyskocil Again a very nice "tips & trick" post on PDF but in V2. Supercool. Thanks for it.

I wish they can make this "renderForm" technique applicable for all services(incl. Lead or Survey) in C4C V2. OR at least start putting your debug sheet into the standard system which can serve as a way to capture the PDF data at runtime (not for PROD system of course).

PS: I recently used your PDF debug sheet for V1, for troubleshooting an issue and I can't tell you how easy it was to capture the data at runtime(without waiting for someone at SAP to provide me XML form data from backend)

best
Saurabh

entry.title: Re: International Trade : PRODCOM in S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @3070856 

 

An app for PRODCOM is not available in Cloud . instead customers use the apps 

mentioned in the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2432527

like "Manage Commodity Codes" and "Classify Products – Commodity Codes"

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Cleared in the meantime"error message
entry.content: Hi Pyae, It looks like the payload is incorrect based on this error message. Please review the sample code provided (halfway through the page) on https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/5142cdd767b04122a8beb6ecd460a922.html

entry.title: Re: Learning Journey – Retail Industry
entry.content:
### Exciting News: New E-learning Courses on SAP Learning Hub!

Attention all SAP business users and implementation consultants! We're thrilled to introduce two new E-learning courses available now on the SAP Learning Hub focused on SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing (SAP OPP).

1. **SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing - Overview**:
Dive deep into the functionalities and strategic applications of SAP OPP. This course offers a comprehensive overview, integrating scenarios, and tips on positioning SAP OPP effectively in the market.
- **Course ID:** ICOPP1_EN_Col01

2. **SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing - Basic Setup**:
Get hands-on with the essential steps to set up SAP Omnichannel Promotion Pricing efficiently. This course is perfect for getting started and handling basic configurations.
- **Course ID:** ICOPP2_EN_Col01

Both courses are designed to enhance your understanding and skills with SAP OPP, ensuring you leverage the software to its full potential. They provide rich content suited for those new to SAP OPP as well as seasoned users looking to refresh or enhance their knowledge.

Log in to your SAP Learning Hub account, find these courses under My Learning/Library, and self-assign them to start boosting your expertise today!

Stay ahead in the game with SAP Learning Hub – your go-to for mastering SAP solutions!

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 3 - Image Embed
entry.content: @Vitaliy-R here is the 3rd week submission, I want to revisit for more challenge.

entry.title: How to get actual GST applicable for the material using ABAP?
entry.content:
Hi,

  I would like to know GST applied to the material code using ABAP. I would like to understand tables from where I can get actual rate e.g. 18% or 12%. I tried searching blogs and as per suggestions checked tax classification using MLAN, TAKM etc. tables but all entries showing 0 value. Is there any way I can find actual rate as 18% etc. As classification based on country code there could be export material also.

Regards,

PD

entry.title: Re: SAP Commercial project management availability in S/4...
entry.content: Start learning about Public Cloud Enterprise Projects here:

entry.title: Re: SAP Commercial project management availability in S/4...
entry.content: Start learning about Public Cloud Enterprise Projects here:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/97e2a4e0c9954d519ae3c862d7b7f8ac.html

entry.title: How to create Z scales for rate tables in TM that can evaluate the material item group in TM
Re: Learning Journey – Public Sector Industry
entry.content:
Welcome to our latest addition to the SAP Learning Hub: E-learning courses focused on Financial Contract Accounting (FI-CA), SAP Public Sector Collections and Disbursements (SAP PSCD), and SAP Tax and Revenue Management (SAP TRM).

Enhance your expertise in the financial domain with our carefully curated Learning Journey including several new courses designed for both business users and implementation consultants. Dive into course options like BR240, IPS510, IPS500, and IPS520 which encompass a range of topics from collection and disbursement to tax administration processes.

Choose from flexible learning formats such as E-books, E-learning modules, and in-depth Learning Classes spanning from 1 to 5 days. Our practice systems allow hands-on experience and self-assessment tests help gauge your understanding effectively.

Do not miss out on these comprehensive courses to boost your SAP knowledge and skills. Enroll today in the SAP Learning Hub and take your first step in mastering Financial Contract Accounting and Tax Management!

Check out the Public Sector Learning Journey (https://help.sap.com/learning-journeys/50021faf7a2610149a0e8d3b2b2278ca) for detailed course offerings and embark on your e-learning expedition now!

Public Sector SAP Public Sector Collection and Disbursement SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: full solr indexing in local - Indexer worker failed....
entry.content:
The exception is due to your product data. You need to remove corrupted products from database, or ignore errors during indexing.

Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "de.hybris.platform.core.model.ItemModel.getPk()" because "itemModel" is null
at de.hybris.platform.solrfacetsearch.indexer.impl.DefaultIndexer.indexItems(DefaultIndexer.java:206) ~[solrfacetsearchserver.jar:?]

entry.title: Re: Save the Date for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
Hi @maria_silipo,

When I get into the Learning Hub, I get the message "This offering is not available or may not be part of an edition of SAP Learning Hub that you have access to. Please click here to download an overview of all current content per edition."
Do you know what's the problem?

BR
Matt
